claims.text: NodeKit can create a deterministic, local-ready agent application from an empty directory and can additively adopt an existing repository.
limitations: Live sponsor research, deployment, and production browser proof are separate gates.

claims.text: NodeAgent now has a proposed provider-neutral runtime contract and real Pi AI adapter rather than requiring a second domain runtime.
limitations: The package is not published and production consumers have not been promoted.

claims.text: NodeSlide now has merged governed core and controlled React packages plus reviewable external-agent and NodeRoom consumer seams.
limitations: The mounted production UI and PPTX export/reopen path are not certified.

claims.text: Under 30 minutes remains a target, not a repeatably certified guarantee.
limitations: Observed dependency installation variance crossed the target.

claims.text: Mature applications can content-bind existing paths, packs, subagents, and evaluations before replacing their runtimes.
limitations: Native runtime shadow parity has not run.

claims.text: NodeTasks supplies a structurally validated 9,155-task evaluation corpus without upgrading proxy tasks into official benchmark scores.
limitations: The corpus receipt is not an application quality score or leaderboard result.

claims.text: BetterPRHandoff can deterministically transform a real archived handoff into NodeKit Present story artifacts and a content-hashed receipt.
limitations: The transformation does not independently re-verify current implementation evidence; direct deck rendering and PPTX export remain separate NodeSlide steps.

claims.text: FeatureClipStudio can deterministically project tracked clips and screenshots into NodeKit's Evidence Index without conflating artifact integrity with product-workflow proof.
limitations: The current assets have no independent browser or judge receipts, so release readiness and product-workflow proof remain uncertified.
